docs.title: Met-R Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for met-r.io — the intelligent documentation orchestration platform by MetaPercept.

Use this site to learn how to convert, manage, publish, and govern technical content at scale.

docs.title: Get started
docs.text: Guide Description Introduction to Met-R What Met-R is and who it is for Prerequisites Accounts, tooling, network, third-party licenses Quick start First conversion in under 15 minutes Key concepts Formats, pipelines, workspaces, and outputs System requirements Browser, network, and supported formats Licensing Plans, entitlements, and usage limits

docs.title: Internal documentation (engineering team)
docs.text: The following content is for MetaPercept engineers, not end-users. It lives in the same GitHub repository under docs/pages/ and poc/ — browse the repository on GitHub for engineering runbooks and POC evaluations.

docs.title: Contributing to docs
docs.text:
See CONTRIBUTING.md for style guide, folder structure, and how to submit documentation changes.

Documentation version: 1.0 · Last updated: June 2026 · docs.met-r.io

docs.title: Contributing to Met-R Documentation
docs.text: Guidelines for writing and publishing documentation for met-r.io, modeled after professional doc sites such as GitHub Docs.

docs.title: Documentation principles
docs.text: Principle Guideline Audience-first Write for the reader's job (author, admin, developer) — not internal jargon Task-oriented Lead with how to accomplish something, not system internals Accurate Verify against the running product; mark unreleased features as Beta Scannable Short paragraphs, tables, numbered steps, clear headings Maintainable One topic per file; cross-link instead of duplicating

docs.title: Folder structure
docs.text:
product-docs/
├── README.md                 ← Hub page (like docs.github.com/en)
├── CONTRIBUTING.md           ← This file
├── mkdocs.yml                ← Site config for docs.met-r.io
├── get-started/              ← Onboarding
├── product/                  ← What Met-R is and does
├── guides/                   ← How-to guides for users
├── admin/                    ← Administration
├── developers/               ← API and integrations
├── reference/                ← Lookup tables, glossary, FAQ
└── support/                  ← Troubleshooting, contact, releases

docs.title: Section naming rules
docs.text: Folder Use for Avoid get-started/ First-time setup, concepts Deep technical reference product/ Feature descriptions, capabilities Step-by-step procedures guides/ Task procedures ("How to…") Marketing copy admin/ Org settings, security, pipelines End-user authoring tasks developers/ API, SDK, webhooks User-facing UI help reference/ Tables, glossary, enums Narrative tutorials support/ Errors, contact, changelog Feature specs

docs.title: File naming
docs.text:
• Lowercase with hyphens: conversion-workflows.md
  • README.md in each folder = section index
  • Keep filenames short and descriptive

docs.title: Page template
docs.text:
# Page title

One-sentence summary of what this page covers.

---

## Prerequisites

- Item 1
- Item 2

---

## Steps (or sections)

### Step 1 — Do something

Explanation and commands.

---

## Related articles

- [Link to related page](../path/page.md)

docs.title: Writing style
docs.text: Do Don't Use active voice: "Click Publish" Passive: "The publish button should be clicked" Use exact UI labels in bold Paraphrase button names Include screenshots for complex UI Walls of text without visuals Note version availability: Available in Met-R 2.x Assume all readers are on latest Link to GitHub Docs patterns when helpful Copy GitHub content verbatim

docs.title: Publishing to docs.met-r.io
docs.text: Documentation is built with MkDocs Material.

docs.title: Local preview
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs-material
cd product-docs
mkdocs serve
# Open http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: Build static site
docs.text:
mkdocs build
# Output in product-docs/site/

docs.title: Deploy
docs.text: Deploy site/ to your hosting target (GitHub Pages, Azure Static Web Apps, S3 + CloudFront, etc.) with custom domain docs.met-r.io.

docs.title: Review process
docs.text:
1. Create a branch from dev
  2. Add or edit markdown under product-docs/
  3. Run mkdocs serve locally and verify links
  4. Open a pull request with label documentation
  5. Maintainer review and merge to main
  6. GitHub Pages workflow publishes the site automatically

docs.title: Status labels
docs.text:
Use in page front matter or opening paragraph:

Label Meaning GA Generally available, production-ready Beta Available but may change Preview Early access, not for production Deprecated Scheduled for removal; link to replacement

docs.title: Questions
docs.text: Contact MetaPercept via admin@metapercept.com or open a GitHub issue in this repository.

docs.title: Governance & security
docs.text: Access control, audit, and compliance for Met-R workspaces.

docs.title: Access control
docs.text:
• Role-based access per workspace
  • API keys scoped to workspace
  • SSO integration (enterprise plans)

docs.title: Content security
docs.text:
• All traffic encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+)
  • Workspace data isolation between tenants
  • Security scanning on all converter pipelines (security-scan check)

docs.title: Audit
docs.text:
• User action logs in Client Admin
  • Conversion and publish job history
  • API access logs via API Gateway

docs.title: Compliance
docs.text:
Contact MetaPercept for:

  • Data residency requirements
  • SOC 2 / ISO documentation
  • Custom BAA or DPA agreements

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Workspace setup
  • Pipeline configuration

docs.title: Pipeline configuration
docs.text: Configure conversion pipelines and CI/CD integration.

docs.title: Pipeline components
docs.text: Component Repository Purpose Shared pipelines metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines Reusable CI/CD templates XSLT Library metR-XSLT-Library Custom transforms DITA validation dita2-validation-transformation Schema validation rules

docs.title: Configure a pipeline
docs.text:
1. Open Client Admin → Pipelines
  2. Select or create a pipeline profile
  3. Add conversion steps (converter service + target format)
  4. Add validation steps (XSLT, DITA-OT schema, security scan)
  5. Set output channel (PDF, HTML5, DocSite)
  6. Save and assign to workspace or project

docs.title: CI/CD integration
docs.text:
Pipelines integrate with GitHub Actions. Required status checks for governance branches:

  • unit-tests-pytest
  • xslt-validation
  • dita-ot-schema-check
  • security-scan

docs.title: API Gateway
docs.text: The Met-R API Gateway (metR-API-Gateway) is the single entry point for all API traffic.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text: Function Description Routing Direct requests to converter, doc, and output services Authentication Validate API keys and tokens Rate limiting Protect services from abuse Logging Request/response audit trail

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Client → API Gateway → Converter / DocManager / Output services

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• API overview
  • Platform overview

docs.title: API overview
docs.text:
Programmatic access to Met-R via REST API through the API Gateway.

Repository: metR-API-Gateway

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
https://api.met-r.io/v1

Your workspace may use a custom API endpoint — check with your administrator.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Method Use case API key Server-to-server integrations Bearer token User-scoped operations OAuth 2.0 Enterprise SSO integrations

Include the token in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN

docs.title: Common endpoints
docs.text:
Endpoint Method Description /documents GET, POST List and upload documents /documents/{id} GET, PUT, DELETE Document CRUD /conversions POST Start a conversion job /conversions/{id} GET Job status and output /publish POST Publish to output channel

Full API reference

OpenAPI specification coming soon. Contact DevOps for early access.

docs.title: XSLT library
docs.text:
Reusable XSLT stylesheets for custom content transformations.

Repository: metR-XSLT-Library

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The XSLT Library provides shared transformation rules used across converter and output services. Extend or override stylesheets for customer-specific formatting requirements.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
1. Admin uploads custom XSLT to workspace
  2. Pipeline references stylesheet by name
  3. Converter applies transform after base conversion
  4. xslt-validation CI check validates stylesheet

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Pipeline configuration
  • Converters

docs.title: Introduction to Met-R
docs.text: Met-R (met-r.io) is MetaPercept's intelligent documentation orchestration platform. It helps enterprises convert, manage, validate, and publish technical content across formats — from legacy authoring tools to modern web, PDF, and CMS delivery.

docs.title: Who is Met-R for?
docs.text: Audience Use Met-R to… Technical writers Author, convert, and publish DITA, DocBook, FrameMaker, and Markdown content Documentation managers Govern workflows, approvals, and multi-channel output DevOps / platform teams Operate conversion pipelines, CI/CD, and infrastructure Developers Integrate via API Gateway, webhooks, and custom XSLT transforms

docs.title: What problems does Met-R solve?
docs.text:
• Format fragmentation — unify content from FrameMaker, DITA, DocBook, Word, PDF, HTML, and CMS sources
  • Manual conversion — automate repeatable transformation pipelines with validation
  • Publishing bottlenecks — deliver PDF, HTML5, CMS, and structured XML/JSON from a single source
  • Quality risk — enforce schema validation, XSLT checks, and review gates before publish
  • AI-ready content — prepare and enrich documentation for AI agents and intelligent search

docs.title: Platform at a glance
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        met-r.io Platform                        │
├─────────────┬─────────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────┤
│  Converters │ Doc Mgmt    │  Publishing │  AI & Intelligence    │
│  FM→DITA    │ DocManager  │  PDF        │  Determinacy Engine   │
│  HTML/MD    │ DocEditor   │  HTML5/CMS  │  AI Agents            │
│  DocBook    │ DocMigrate  │  XML/JSON   │  Master AI Agent      │
├─────────────┴─────────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────┤
│  API Gateway  ·  Client Admin  ·  Server Admin  ·  Infinity DocSite │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Key concepts
docs.text: Core terminology and concepts used across the Met-R platform.

docs.title: Workspace
docs.text: A workspace is your organization's isolated environment on Met-R. It contains users, documents, pipelines, and output channels. Each customer typically has one or more workspaces.

docs.title: Source format vs target format
docs.text:
Term Definition Examples Source format The authoring format you upload FrameMaker, DITA, DocBook, Markdown, DOCX, PDF Target format The output Met-R produces HTML5, PDF, DITA, CMS package, XML/JSON

See the full matrix in Supported formats.

docs.title: Converter
docs.text:
A converter is a microservice that transforms content between formats. Each converter is maintained as an independent service in the Met-R platform.

Converter Repository Source → Target FrameMaker metR-Converter-FrameMaker FM → DITA HTML metR-Converter-HTML HTML ↔ structured formats Markdown metR-Converter-Markdown MD ↔ structured formats DOCX metR-Converter-DOCX Word → structured formats DocBook metR-Converter-DocBook DocBook ↔ DITA/HTML PropDITA metR-Converter-PropDITA Proprietary DITA variants Sitecore XML metR-Converter-SitecoreXML Sitecore → standard formats PDF metR-Converter-PDF PDF extraction / conversion

docs.title: Pipeline
docs.text:
A pipeline defines the sequence of conversion, validation, and publishing steps applied to a document. Pipelines can include:

  • Format conversion
  • XSLT transformation (metR-XSLT-Library)
  • DITA-OT schema validation
  • Security scan
  • Output to PDF, HTML5, or CMS

Pipelines are configured in Client Admin or via CI/CD (metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines).

docs.title: Document lifecycle
docs.text:
Upload → Convert → Validate → Review → Publish → Deliver
Stage Component Upload DocManager Edit DocEditor Migrate DocMigrate Publish DocPublisher Deliver Infinity DocSite, HTML5/CMS output

docs.title: Output channels
docs.text: Channel Description PDF Print-ready output (metR-Output-PDF) HTML5 Web-ready HTML (metR-Output-HTML5-CMS) XML/JSON AI Structured output for AI consumption (metR-Output-XML-JSON-AI) Infinity DocSite Dynamic documentation portal (metR-InfinityDocSite)

docs.title: AI capabilities
docs.text:
Met-R includes AI-powered features for intelligent documentation workflows:

Feature Description Determinacy Engine Structured decision logic for content processing Master AI Agent Orchestrates multi-step documentation tasks AI-assisted conversion FrameMaker-to-DITA and similar agent-driven flows

See AI capabilities.

docs.title: Administration roles
docs.text: Role Access Author Upload, edit, convert, request publish Reviewer Approve content before publication Client Admin Workspace users, pipelines, client settings Server Admin Infrastructure, services, server-level config

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Everything required before using Met-R — accounts, tooling, network, and third-party licenses.

docs.title: Before you begin
docs.text: Prerequisite Required for How to obtain Met-R workspace account All users Invited by workspace admin or MetaPercept Supported web browser Web UI Install latest Chrome, Edge, or Firefox Network access to *.met-r.io Web UI, API IT firewall allowlist Source authoring tool (if applicable) Upload & convert See Authoring tool licenses API credentials (optional) Integrations Issued by workspace admin

docs.title: Workspace access
docs.text:
1. Your organization must have an active Met-R subscription (see Licensing)
  2. A Client Admin creates your user account and assigns a role:
  3. Viewer, Author, Reviewer, Client Admin, or Server Admin
  4. You receive an invitation email with sign-in instructions

docs.title: Role minimums by task
docs.text: Task Minimum role View published content Viewer Upload and convert documents Author Approve for publish Reviewer Manage users and pipelines Client Admin Server / infrastructure config Server Admin

docs.title: Web browser
docs.text:
Browser Minimum Google Chrome Latest 2 major versions Microsoft Edge Latest 2 major versions Mozilla Firefox Latest 2 major versions Safari Latest 2 major versions (macOS)

Requirements: JavaScript enabled, cookies allowed, TLS 1.2+.

docs.title: Network
docs.text: Requirement Detail Protocol HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher) Outbound URLs *.met-r.io, your workspace URL Ports 443 (HTTPS) Proxy Standard HTTPS proxy supported; contact admin for config Upload bandwidth Minimum 5 Mbps recommended for large book files

docs.title: Hardware (author workstation)
docs.text: Component Minimum Recommended CPU 4 cores 8+ cores RAM 8 GB 16 GB Disk 10 GB free SSD with 50 GB+ for local authoring tools Display 1280 × 720 1920 × 1080

docs.title: Authoring tool licenses
docs.text:
Met-R converts content from your existing authoring tools. You may need valid licenses for source tools depending on your workflow.

Tool Required when License type Notes Adobe FrameMaker Uploading .fm / .book files Commercial (Adobe) Met-R converts FM → DITA; FM not required to view output Microsoft Word Uploading .docx Microsoft 365 or standalone DOCX conversion only Oxygen XML Editor Optional — editing DITA locally Commercial (Oxygen) Not required for Met-R web UI DITA Open Toolkit N/A for authors Included in platform Used server-side by Met-R Markdown editor Optional Any (VS Code, etc.) Free options available

Server-side processing

DITA-OT, XSLT engines, and conversion pipelines run on Met-R infrastructure. Authors do not need to install DITA-OT locally unless doing offline development.

docs.title: API integration prerequisites
docs.text:
For REST API access via API Gateway:

Requirement Detail API key or OAuth token Issued by Client Admin HTTPS client TLS 1.2+ JSON support Request and response bodies Rate limit awareness Standard: 60 req/min (see API overview)

docs.title: On-premises / dedicated deployment
docs.text:
Additional prerequisites for private or dedicated deployments:

Requirement Detail Kubernetes cluster 1.27+ Container registry Accessible from cluster DNS & TLS certificate Custom domain for workspace Identity provider SAML/OIDC for SSO (enterprise) Storage Object storage for document files

Contact MetaPercept sales for the full infrastructure checklist and engineering prerequisites.

docs.title: Pre-flight checklist
docs.text:
☐ Workspace account created and invitation accepted
☐ Browser updated and JavaScript enabled
☐ Network access to met-r.io confirmed
☐ Source files prepared in supported format
☐ Authoring tool license valid (if using FM, Word, etc.)
☐ Role assigned (Author or above for conversions)
☐ API key obtained (if integrating programmatically)

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text: Get your first document conversion running on Met-R in under 15 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Met-R workspace account with Author role or above
  • Supported browser (Chrome, Edge, or Firefox — latest 2 versions)
  • Network access to *.met-r.io
  • Source file in a supported format
  • Active subscription (see Licensing)

Full checklist: Prerequisites

docs.title: Step 1 — Sign in
docs.text:
1. Go to met-r.io (or your organization's Met-R URL)
  2. Sign in with your workspace credentials
  3. Open the Dashboard

docs.title: Step 2 — Upload source content
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Documents → Upload
  2. Select your source file
  3. Choose the source format (e.g. FrameMaker, DITA, Markdown)
  4. Click Upload

docs.title: Step 3 — Run a conversion
docs.text:
1. Open the uploaded document
  2. Click Convert
  3. Select target format (e.g. DITA, HTML5, PDF)
  4. Choose a conversion profile (or use the default pipeline)
  5. Click Run conversion

Met-R queues the job and runs it through the appropriate converter service.

docs.title: Step 4 — Review output
docs.text:
1. When status shows Complete, open the Output tab
  2. Preview HTML or download PDF/XML
  3. Review validation results (schema check, XSLT validation)

docs.title: Step 5 — Publish (optional)
docs.text:
1. Click Publish
  2. Select target channel: Infinity DocSite, CMS, or file download
  3. Confirm publication

docs.title: What's next?
docs.text:
Goal Read Understand formats and pipelines Key concepts Configure admin settings Workspace setup Integrate via API API overview Troubleshoot errors Troubleshooting

Need a demo environment?

Contact your MetaPercept account manager or see Contact support.

docs.title: System requirements
docs.text: Hardware, software, and network requirements for using Met-R.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before using Met-R, ensure you have:

  • Active workspace account (see Prerequisites)
  • Supported browser and network access
  • Valid subscription plan (see Licensing)
  • Source authoring tool license if uploading FrameMaker, Word, etc.

docs.title: Browser requirements
docs.text:
Browser Minimum version Google Chrome Latest 2 major versions Microsoft Edge Latest 2 major versions Mozilla Firefox Latest 2 major versions Safari Latest 2 major versions (macOS)

JavaScript must be enabled. Pop-up blockers may interfere with file downloads.

docs.title: Network
docs.text: Requirement Detail HTTPS All traffic over TLS 1.2+ Outbound access *.met-r.io and your workspace URL File upload size Configurable per workspace (default: contact admin) API access REST over HTTPS via API Gateway

docs.title: Supported source formats
docs.text:
Format Extensions Notes Adobe FrameMaker .fm, .book FM → DITA conversion DITA .dita, .ditamap DITA 1.2 / 1.3 DocBook .xml DocBook 5.x Markdown .md CommonMark / GFM Microsoft Word .docx DOCX conversion HTML .html, .htm Structured HTML PDF .pdf Extraction workflows

Full matrix: Supported formats.

docs.title: API client requirements
docs.text:
For programmatic access via API Gateway:

  • HTTPS client with TLS 1.2+
  • API key or OAuth token (issued by workspace admin)
  • JSON request/response support

docs.title: On-premises / private cloud
docs.text: For self-hosted or dedicated deployments, contact MetaPercept for infrastructure requirements (Kubernetes, storage, compute minimums).

docs.title: Author workflows
docs.text: How content authors create, edit, and prepare documentation for publication.

docs.title: Create new content
docs.text:
1. Upload — add source file (FM, DITA, MD, DOCX)
  2. Edit — open in DocEditor for in-platform changes
  3. Save version — DocManager tracks version history

docs.title: Edit existing content
docs.text:
1. Open document in DocEditor
  2. Make changes with autosave
  3. Add change summary for reviewers
  4. Submit for review when ready

docs.title: Best practices
docs.text:
• Use consistent file naming conventions
  • Add metadata tags at upload time
  • Run validation before submitting for review
  • Link related topics in DITA maps

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Review & approval
  • Document management

docs.title: Conversion workflows
docs.text: End-to-end procedures for converting content between formats.

docs.title: Standard conversion
docs.text:
1. Upload source file with correct format selected
  2. Choose target format and pipeline profile
  3. Run conversion — monitor job status
  4. Review validation report
  5. Download or publish output

docs.title: FrameMaker → DITA
docs.text:
1. Upload .fm or .book file
  2. Select FrameMaker source, DITA target
  3. Choose FM→DITA pipeline (standard or AI-assisted)
  4. Review DITA topic structure in output preview
  5. Fix validation errors and re-run if needed

See FrameMaker converter.

docs.title: Bulk migration
docs.text:
For large-scale legacy migration:

  1. Admin configures DocMigrate job
  2. Batch upload source files
  3. Pipeline runs conversions in parallel
  4. Review aggregate validation report
  5. Publish to target channel

docs.title: Pipeline validation
docs.text:
Every conversion runs these checks (where applicable):

Check Description unit-tests-pytest Service unit tests xslt-validation XSLT transform validation dita-ot-schema-check DITA schema compliance security-scan Dependency and content security

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Converters
  • Supported formats

docs.title: Review & approval
docs.text: Quality gates before content is published to production channels.

docs.title: Review workflow
docs.text: Author submits → Reviewer assigned → Feedback / Approve → Publish

docs.title: For authors
docs.text:
1. Complete conversion and fix validation errors
  2. Click Submit for review
  3. Add summary of changes
  4. Address reviewer feedback and resubmit if needed

docs.title: For reviewers
docs.text:
1. Open review queue in Dashboard
  2. Compare source and output
  3. Check validation report
  4. Approve or Request changes with comments

docs.title: Approval requirements
docs.text:
Workspace admins can configure:

  • Minimum number of reviewers
  • Required reviewer roles
  • Auto-publish on approval vs manual publish

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Author workflows
  • Publishing
  • Admin governance

docs.title: Infinity DocSite
docs.text: Infinity DocSite (metR-InfinityDocSite) delivers a dynamic, searchable documentation portal for your product content.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Infinity DocSite publishes validated Met-R content as a branded documentation website with search, navigation, and version support.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Feature Description Full-text search Search across all published topics Versioned content Publish and browse multiple product versions Responsive design Mobile and desktop optimized Custom branding Workspace logo, colors, and domain Access control Public or authenticated access per workspace

docs.title: Publishing to Infinity DocSite
docs.text:
1. Complete conversion and review workflow
  2. In DocPublisher, select Infinity DocSite as output channel
  3. Configure site map and navigation
  4. Publish — site updates within minutes

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Publishing & output
  • Admin — workspace setup

docs.title: Platform overview
docs.text: Met-R is a modular documentation orchestration platform. Each capability is delivered as an independent service, enabling scalable conversion, management, and publishing workflows.

docs.title: Core services
docs.text: Service Repository Purpose Frontend metR-Frontend Web UI for authors and admins API Gateway metR-API-Gateway API routing, auth, rate limiting Client Admin metR-Client-Admin Workspace and client configuration Server Admin metR-Server-Admin Server and infrastructure administration Core Infrastructure metR-Core-Infrastructure Shared platform infrastructure

docs.title: Converter services
docs.text:
See Converters for full details.

Service Input Output FrameMaker .fm DITA, HTML HTML HTML DITA, Markdown Markdown .md HTML, DITA DOCX .docx DITA, HTML DocBook DocBook XML DITA, HTML PropDITA Proprietary DITA Standard DITA Sitecore XML Sitecore DITA, HTML PDF .pdf Structured content

docs.text:
Trigger (upload / schedule / API)
    → Master AI Agent plans steps
    → Converter + Determinacy Engine execute
    → Validation + human review gate
    → Publish to output channel

docs.title: Governance
docs.text: AI features respect workspace access controls. Sensitive content stays within your workspace boundary. Contact your admin for data residency and model usage policies.

docs.title: Converters
docs.text: Met-R converter services transform content between authoring formats. Each converter runs as an independent microservice with dedicated validation and CI pipelines.

docs.title: Converter catalog
docs.text: Converter Repository Primary use case FrameMaker metR-Converter-FrameMaker Legacy FM books → DITA HTML metR-Converter-HTML HTML normalization and transformation Markdown metR-Converter-Markdown MD ↔ structured formats DOCX metR-Converter-DOCX Microsoft Word → DITA/HTML DocBook metR-Converter-DocBook DocBook ↔ DITA PropDITA metR-Converter-PropDITA Proprietary DITA dialect normalization Sitecore XML metR-Converter-SitecoreXML Sitecore CMS export conversion PDF metR-Converter-PDF PDF content extraction

docs.title: Common conversion flow
docs.text:
Source file → Upload → Converter service → XSLT / validation → Target output
  1. Author uploads source file via UI or API
  2. API Gateway routes to the appropriate converter
  3. Converter applies transformation rules
  4. XSLT Library post-processing (if configured)
  5. Schema validation (DITA-OT, custom rules)
  6. Output delivered to publishing channel

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
All converters run through standard CI checks:

Check Context Unit tests unit-tests-pytest XSLT validation xslt-validation DITA schema dita-ot-schema-check Security scan security-scan

docs.text:

docs.title: DocBook converter
docs.text:
Transform content between DocBook and DITA.

Repository: metR-Converter-DocBook Status: GA

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The DocBook converter supports migration and interoperability between DocBook XML and DITA-based content sets.

docs.title: Supported inputs
docs.text: Input Extensions DocBook XML .xml

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Converters overview
  • Supported formats

docs.title: DOCX converter
docs.text:
Convert Microsoft Word documents to DITA and HTML.

Repository: metR-Converter-DOCX Status: GA

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The DOCX converter migrates Word .docx files into structured topics while preserving headings, lists, tables, and cross-references where possible.

docs.title: Supported inputs
docs.text: Input Extensions Microsoft Word .docx

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Converters overview
  • Prerequisites

docs.title: FrameMaker converter
docs.text:
Convert Adobe FrameMaker content to DITA and other structured formats.

Repository: metR-Converter-FrameMaker Status: GA

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The FrameMaker converter migrates legacy .fm and book files into structured DITA content, preserving topic structure, cross-references, and conditional processing attributes where possible.

docs.title: Supported inputs
docs.text: Input Extensions FrameMaker document .fm FrameMaker book .book

docs.title: Supported outputs
docs.text: Output Format DITA topics .dita DITA map .ditamap HTML (intermediate) .html

docs.title: AI-assisted conversion
docs.text: FrameMaker-to-DITA workflows support AI agent-assisted conversion for complex legacy structures. See AI capabilities.

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Conversion workflows
  • Converters overview

docs.title: HTML converter
docs.text:
Normalize and transform structured HTML content for downstream publishing.

Repository: metR-Converter-HTML Status: GA

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The HTML converter ingests structured HTML and applies Met-R transformation rules for migration into DITA, DocBook, or CMS-ready output.

docs.title: Supported inputs
docs.text: Input Extensions HTML document .html, .htm

docs.title: PropDITA converter

Normalize proprietary DITA dialects to standard DITA.

Repository: metR-Converter-PropDITA Status: GA

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The PropDITA converter standardizes custom or legacy DITA variants so they validate against DITA Open Toolkit rules and Met-R publishing pipelines.

docs.text: The Sitecore XML converter processes CMS export packages for migration into DITA, HTML, or other Met-R publishing targets.

docs.text:
1. Content passes validation and review gates
  2. DocPublisher selects output profile
  3. Output service generates deliverable
  4. Content delivered to channel (download, CMS push, DocSite)

docs.text: Channel Best for PDF Print, regulated industries, offline distribution HTML5 Web help, responsive documentation CMS Sitecore, custom CMS integration XML/JSON AI RAG, search, AI agent consumption Infinity DocSite Searchable product documentation portal

docs.title: FAQ
docs.text: Frequently asked questions about Met-R.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
What is Met-R? Met-R is MetaPercept's documentation orchestration platform for converting, managing, and publishing technical content. See Introduction.

Who is Met-R for? Technical writers, documentation managers, DevOps teams, and developers working with structured content at scale.

docs.title: Formats & conversion
docs.text:
What formats does Met-R support? See Supported formats for the full matrix.

Can Met-R convert FrameMaker to DITA? Yes. See FrameMaker converter.

docs.title: Access & security
docs.text:
How do I get workspace access? Contact your organization administrator or MetaPercept account manager.

Is my content secure? Yes. Content is isolated per workspace with TLS encryption. See Governance.

docs.title: API
docs.text: Is there an API? Yes. See API overview.

docs.title: Support
docs.text: How do I report a bug? See Contact support.

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Troubleshooting
  • Glossary

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Met-R platform terminology.

Term Definition Converter Microservice that transforms content between formats DITA Darwin Information Typing Architecture — XML standard for technical docs DocSite See Infinity DocSite Determinacy Engine Rule-based engine for content processing decisions DITA-OT DITA Open Toolkit — industry-standard publishing engine Infinity DocSite Met-R dynamic documentation portal Pipeline Sequence of conversion, validation, and publish steps PropDITA Proprietary DITA dialect requiring normalization Workspace Isolated tenant environment on Met-R XSLT Extensible Stylesheet Language Transformations

docs.title: Licensing
docs.text: Met-R subscription plans, entitlements, and third-party license requirements.

docs.title: Met-R product plans
docs.text:
Feature Starter Professional Enterprise Users Up to 5 Up to 25 Unlimited Workspaces 1 3 Unlimited Converters Core (HTML, MD, DOCX) All converters All + custom Output channels PDF, HTML5 + CMS, XML/JSON AI + Infinity DocSite, custom AI features — Determinacy Engine Full AI agent suite API access — Standard API Full API + webhooks SSO / SAML — — Included Dedicated instance — — Optional Support Email (P4) Email (P3) Priority + SLA Billing Monthly Annual Custom contract

Pricing

Contact MetaPercept sales or your account manager for current pricing. Plans and limits are subject to change.

docs.title: What is included in every plan
docs.text: Included Description Platform hosting SaaS at met-r.io (unless dedicated) TLS encryption In transit for all traffic DITA-OT processing Server-side — no separate DITA-OT license needed XSLT transforms Platform XSLT library usage Workspace isolation Data separated per workspace Standard CI validation Schema and XSLT checks on conversions Platform updates Security patches and feature releases

docs.title: Usage limits (typical)
docs.text:
Resource Starter Professional Enterprise Conversion jobs / month 500 5,000 Custom Storage 10 GB 100 GB Custom API requests / min — 60 Custom Infinity DocSite sites — 1 Unlimited Concurrent conversion jobs 2 10 Custom

Overage billing or upgrade required when limits are exceeded. Admins receive usage alerts at 80% and 100%.

docs.title: User license types
docs.text:
License Assigned to Counted as Viewer Read-only users 1 seat Author Content creators 1 seat Reviewer Approvers 1 seat Admin Client / Server Admin 1 seat

A user with multiple roles counts as one seat.

docs.title: Third-party licenses (customer responsibility)
docs.text:
Met-R does not include licenses for third-party authoring tools. Customers must maintain:

Software License owner Required for Adobe FrameMaker Customer FM source file authoring Microsoft Word / 365 Customer DOCX authoring Oxygen XML Author Customer (optional) Advanced DITA editing offline Sitecore Customer CMS source content Atlassian (Jira/Confluence) Customer (optional) Integration only

Included in Met-R subscription (no separate customer license):

  • DITA Open Toolkit (server-side)
  • Platform XSLT library
  • PDF generation engine (server-side)
  • AI model usage (within plan limits)

docs.title: AI usage licensing
docs.text:
Plan AI entitlement Starter Not included Professional Determinacy Engine; limited agent calls/month Enterprise Full Master AI Agent; custom usage pool

AI usage beyond plan limits is billed per consumption unit or requires plan upgrade. See your workspace admin for current quotas.

docs.title: Enterprise add-ons
docs.text: Add-on Description Dedicated instance Single-tenant cloud deployment Private cloud / on-prem Customer-managed Kubernetes Custom converter Bespoke format support Premium SLA 99.9% uptime, 1-hour P1 response HIPAA / compliance package BAA, enhanced audit, data residency Training & onboarding Admin and author training sessions

docs.title: License compliance
docs.text:
Rule Detail Seat count Must not exceed subscribed user limit Workspace count Per plan tier Sharing credentials Prohibited — one account per person API keys Scoped to workspace; not transferable Audit MetaPercept may audit usage for compliance

Violations may result in service suspension. Contact your account manager to upgrade.

docs.title: Open source components
docs.text:
Met-R platform services use open source software. Key components:

Component License Usage DITA Open Toolkit Apache 2.0 DITA processing Python runtime PSF Converter services Various JS/TS libs MIT / Apache Frontend

A full SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) is available for Enterprise customers on request.

docs.title: Engineering tooling (internal)
docs.text: MetaPercept engineering team tooling is documented in the repository under docs/pages/ (engineering audience only).

docs.title: MetaPercept support channels
docs.text: Channel Use for Email support@metapercept.com Account manager Billing, licensing, new workspaces Enterprise support Platform incidents (Enterprise plan customers)

docs.title: Before contacting support
docs.text:
1. Note your workspace name and user email
  2. Capture the job ID or error message
  3. Check Troubleshooting and FAQ

docs.title: Severity levels
docs.text: Level Description Response target P1 — Critical Platform down, data loss risk 1 hour P2 — High Major feature unavailable 4 hours P3 — Medium Degraded performance, workaround exists 1 business day P4 — Low Questions, minor issues 2 business days

  • Subscribe to MetaPercept product updates via your account manager

Contributing release notes

Add entries here when shipping customer-visible changes. Follow CONTRIBUTING.md.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues and resolutions.

docs.title: Conversion failures
docs.text: Symptom Likely cause Fix Job stuck in Queued Service unavailable Retry; contact admin if persists Validation failed Schema or XSLT error Review validation report; fix source content Unsupported format Wrong format selected at upload Re-upload with correct source format Timeout Large file or complex book Split into smaller files; contact admin

docs.title: Access issues
docs.text: Symptom Fix Cannot sign in Verify credentials; contact workspace admin Permission denied on action Request role upgrade from Client Admin API 401 Unauthorized Regenerate API key; check token expiry

docs.title: Output issues
docs.text: Symptom Fix PDF missing images Check source image references and paths HTML broken layout Review XSLT profile; check CSS configuration DITA validation errors Fix source structure; see validation log
